slides.title: A Different Type of Story
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 5 English, Unit 6: A Different Type of Story. In this unit you will explore the many different types of stories that writers create. Every story belongs to a particular genre, which is the French word for type or kind. By the end of this unit you will understand several genres, recognise their special features, and be able to choose the right genre for your own writing. You will also strengthen your grammar by learning how to connect ideas smoothly using connectives and conjunctions. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Summarise the central ideas and important details in A Different Type of Story texts
Support inferences and comparisons with relevant text evidence
Use Grade 5 vocabulary accurately in discussion and writing
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to summarise the central ideas and important details in a different type of story texts. you will also support inferences and comparisons with relevant text evidence. you will also use grade 5 vocabulary accurately in discussion and writing. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: unrest, uneasy, unexpected, unsuccessful, unfamiliar, uncertain. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: A Different Type of Story: source text 1
A Different Type of Story: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes A Different Type of Story: source text 1 and A Different Type of Story: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: What Are Connectives and Conjunctions?
bullets: A connective is any word or phrase that links ideas in your writing. A conjunction is a specific type of connective that joins words, phrases, or clauses...
slides.narration: The first language focus is What Are Connectives and Conjunctions?. A connective is any word or phrase that links ideas in your writing. A conjunction is a specific type of connective that joins words, phrases, or clauses within a sentence. People often use the two words to mean the same thing. Why do they matter? Without connectives, your writing would be a list of short, choppy sentences. Connectives make your ideas flow and help the reader follow your thinking. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Genre Detective Game. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
